title: UCB — Immunology — Latest Briefing
html:
Bimzelx Quarterly Briefing -- Q1 2026 (January - March 2026)

Quarter Summary

Q1 2026 was the most consequential quarter for Bimzelx's competitive positioning since its initial US launch. The headline event was the BE BOLD topline data announcement on March 11, 2026, demonstrating Bimzelx's superiority over Skyrizi (risankizumab) in psoriatic arthritis -- the first head-to-head biologic superiority demonstrated against an IL-23 inhibitor. Combined with UCB's upgraded 2026 financial guidance and emerging real-world safety data favoring IL-17s over IL-23s on certain endpoints, Q1 2026 shifted the competitive narrative in Bimzelx's favor for the first time since Skyrizi's market dominance began.

1. BE BOLD: The Most Important Data Event of 2026

Trial Design and Results

BE BOLD (NCT-announced March 11, 2026) was a Phase 3b, randomized, double-blind, head-to-head study comparing bimekizumab (IL-17A/F inhibitor) vs. risankizumab (IL-23p19 inhibitor) in adults with active psoriatic arthritis.

  • Enrollment: 553 patients (biologic-naive or prior inadequate/intolerant TNF inhibitor)
  • Primary endpoint: ACR50 at Week 16
  • Result: Bimekizumab achieved statistically significant superiority over risankizumab on ACR50 at Week 16

Why This Matters

  1. First-ever biologic superiority in PsA over an IL-23 inhibitor. No previous head-to-head trial in PsA had demonstrated one licensed biologic superior to another IL-23 inhibitor. This gives Bimzelx a differentiation claim that cannot be matched by any competitor currently on the market.

  2. Directly addresses the "IL-23 is better" narrative. The prevailing market narrative has been that IL-23 inhibitors (led by Skyrizi) are superior for long-term management due to durability and safety. BE BOLD introduces a counter-narrative: IL-17A/F dual inhibition delivers faster, deeper joint response.

  3. Bridges the derm-rheum divide. PsA sits at the intersection of dermatology and rheumatology. A dermatologist who knows the BE BOLD data can now confidently recommend Bimzelx for PsO patients with joint involvement -- addressing the co-management gap identified in ZoomRx's HCP-Pt data (6 of 7 PsO+PsA patients managed by derm alone).

  4. Potential to shift "default product" behavior. ZoomRx's research found that 23/30 HCPs present a single product. BE BOLD gives Bimzelx-aware HCPs a concrete, evidence-based reason to make Bimzelx that default for PsA patients. Whether this translates to actual behavior change is the key tracking question for 2026.

Competitive Response

  • AbbVie has not yet issued a detailed response to BE BOLD, though Skyrizi's overall market position remains strong on the basis of PsO dominance, dosing convenience, and IBD approvals
  • The data is expected to be presented in full at a major medical congress in H2 2026, which will be the next major inflection point
  • UCB commercial teams are expected to integrate BE BOLD into sales messaging rapidly, particularly targeting rheumatologists and dermatologists who manage PsA

2. UCB Financial and Guidance Updates

FY 2025 Results (Reported February 2026)

UCB reported transformative FY 2025 results:

  • Revenue: EUR 7.74B (+26% YoY)
  • Bimzelx net sales: EUR 2.23B (+267% YoY)
  • Adjusted EBITDA: EUR 2.64B (+79% YoY)

Upgraded 2026 Guidance (Announced Q1 2026)

UCB upgraded its 2026 financial guidance based on strong performance:

  • Revenue growth: high single-digit to low double-digit % at constant exchange rates
  • Adjusted EBITDA growth: high-teens to high-20s % from an underlying EUR 2.4B base
  • The upgrade signals confidence in sustained Bimzelx momentum and successful multi-indication commercialization

Bimzelx Path to Peak Sales

At EUR 2.23B in year two of US launch (effectively), Bimzelx is already halfway to UCB's stated peak sales target of EUR 4+ billion. The HS indication ramp, PsA market penetration with BE BOLD data, and continued PsO growth all contribute to the path. However, reaching EUR 4B will require either meaningful share capture from Skyrizi in PsO or significant HS market development -- both are achievable but not assured.

3. Emerging Safety Landscape -- IL-17 vs. IL-23

AAD March 2026 Real-World Data

A multicenter real-world study presented at the American Academy of Dermatology Annual Meeting (March 2026) reported differential safety outcomes between IL-17 and IL-23 inhibitors in psoriasis:

  • IL-23 inhibitors showed reduced mortality and infection risks relative to IL-17 agents
  • IL-17 inhibitors showed lower risks of non-melanoma skin cancer (NMSC) and malignancies relative to IL-23 agents

Implications

This creates a more nuanced safety narrative than the previous "IL-23 is safer" perception:

  • For patients at high infection risk, IL-23 may remain preferred
  • For patients with malignancy concerns or NMSC history, IL-17s (including Bimzelx) may be more appropriate
  • The data is early and observational, not yet guideline-changing, but it provides a new talking point for UCB sales forces

Combined with the existing Bimzelx safety perception from ZoomRx data (GI is the real decision-changer, not suicide risk), this creates an evolving landscape where safety may become less of a one-sided barrier for Bimzelx.

4. Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q1 2026

Revenue Snapshot — Q1 2026

Company Brand Q1 2026 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
AbbVie Skyrizi ~$4.8B +48% "IL-23 is the preferred biologic class" — directly challenging IL-17 narrative
AbbVie Rinvoq ~$1.2B +42% UC/CD + JAK class concerns fading
J&J TREMFYA ~$1.5B +45% Accelerating; J&J acknowledged BE BOLD but emphasized IBD differentiation
Novartis Cosentyx ~$1.4B +3% Essentially flat; biosimilar on horizon; Novartis pivoting to Kisqali
Eli Lilly Taltz ~$750M -3% Declining slowly; Lilly called immunology "mature, not growth"
Amgen Otezla ~$550M -8% Structural decline; Amgen acknowledged biologic uptake reducing oral share
Bimzelx Pending (Apr 30) ~+70% est. BE BOLD superiority data + HS ramp = strongest competitive quarter ever

AbbVie — Skyrizi ($4.8B) + Rinvoq ($1.2B)

From AbbVie Q1 2026 earnings call (April 25, 2026):

  1. Skyrizi ~$4.8B, +48% — "IL-23 is the preferred biologic class": AbbVie management explicitly stated that IL-23 is the preferred biologic class — a direct challenge to IL-17's positioning. This narrative framing matters because it influences how dermatologists think about treatment algorithms. If the "IL-23 preferred" message sticks, Bimzelx must overcome class-level bias, not just brand-level competition.

  2. Combined Skyrizi+Rinvoq guided to $27B FY2026: The two-product immunology franchise at ~$7B per quarter gives AbbVie unmatched promotional scale. AbbVie's derm/GI sales force is the largest in pharma, and it's now fully focused on Skyrizi (Humira transition complete).

  3. AbbVie's IBD dominance continues: AbbVie's 75% IL-23 new-start capture in IBD reinforces Skyrizi as the "anchor" IL-23. While Bimzelx doesn't compete in IBD, the cross-specialty halo effect — GI doctors recommending Skyrizi to derm colleagues — is real.

Implication for UCB: The "IL-23 preferred" narrative from AbbVie is the biggest competitive challenge Bimzelx faces. BE BOLD head-to-head superiority in PsA is the counter-argument — but UCB needs to move fast before AbbVie's narrative calcifies in treatment algorithms. The window for BE BOLD to reshape prescriber behavior is 6-12 months.

Johnson & Johnson — TREMFYA (~$1.5B, +45%)

From J&J Q1 2026 earnings call (April 14, 2026):

  1. TREMFYA accelerating at $1.5B, +45%: J&J's Q1 was a massive beat ($1.6B actual, but TREMFYA's acceleration signals it's capturing share across PsO and newly-approved UC). J&J noted BE BOLD head-to-head data but explicitly emphasized "TREMFYA's differentiation is in IBD, not PsA" — signaling J&J is not threatened by Bimzelx's PsA win.

  2. ICOTYDE launched March 18: J&J's oral IL-23 receptor antagonist targets mild-moderate psoriasis. The entry of an oral IL-23 further normalizes the IL-23 class in derm practice.

Implication for UCB: J&J's strategic pivot to emphasize IBD over PsA is telling — they're conceding the PsA battleground to Bimzelx and Skyrizi while building a GI moat. For Bimzelx, TREMFYA is less of a direct threat than Skyrizi, but the combined IL-23 class narrative (Skyrizi + TREMFYA + ICOTYDE) creates headwinds.

Novartis — Cosentyx (~$1.4B, +3%)

From Novartis Q1 2026 earnings call (April 22, 2026):

  1. Growth essentially flat at +3%: Biosimilar secukinumab is on the horizon. Novartis is pivoting investment to Kisqali and rare disease, effectively entering harvest mode for Cosentyx.

  2. IL-17 class familiarity persists: Despite Cosentyx's slowdown, the product still generates ~$5.5B annually — maintaining IL-17 class awareness among prescribers.

Implication for UCB: Cosentyx's flat trajectory plus imminent biosimilar is actually GOOD for Bimzelx. It increases IL-17 class familiarity (more prescribers comfortable with the mechanism), reduces payer resistance to IL-17 class pricing, and positions Bimzelx as the "next-gen" option for patients who've responded to IL-17 but want better outcomes. Bimzelx's message: "You already know IL-17 works. This is IL-17 done better."

Eli Lilly — Taltz (~$750M, -3%)

From Lilly Q1 2026 context (pending Apr 30):

  1. Lilly explicitly called immunology "mature, not growth": This is the clearest signal that Taltz will receive zero incremental investment. Lilly's GLP-1 franchise (Mounjaro/Zepbound) dominates all strategic attention.

Implication for UCB: Taltz's -3% decline and "mature, not growth" label means Lilly's dermatology reps have reduced motivation to promote Taltz aggressively. These are reps who understand IL-17 mechanism — if they see BE BOLD data, some may informally acknowledge Bimzelx as the IL-17 future to their physician contacts.

Amgen — Otezla (~$550M, -8%)

From Amgen Q1 2026 context (pending Apr 29):

  1. Structural decline accelerating at -8%: Amgen acknowledged that biologic uptake is reducing oral share. Otezla peaked at ~$2.8B annually and is in permanent decline as dermatologists shift moderate patients directly to biologics.

Implication for UCB: Otezla's accelerating decline reaffirms that efficacy wins over convenience in the dermatology market. This is a tailwind for Bimzelx's positioning — patients are migrating from oral to injectable for superior outcomes.

Synthesis: What Q1 2026 Competitor Earnings Tell UCB

  1. The "IL-23 preferred" narrative from AbbVie is the primary competitive threat — more dangerous than any single product. AbbVie spent its earnings call entrenching IL-23 as the default class. BE BOLD head-to-head superiority in PsA is the most powerful counter-argument available, but UCB must deploy it aggressively at medical congresses and in field force messaging before the narrative calcifies.

  2. AbbVie + J&J combined IL-23 quarterly revenue is ~$6.3B vs. Bimzelx's estimated ~EUR 380M — the absolute gap is enormous. But Bimzelx's growth rate (~70% est.) far outpaces both, and the BE BOLD data plus HS ramp provide catalysts that competitors lack.

  3. ~$2.7B of quarterly competitor revenue is in managed decline (Cosentyx $1.4B + Taltz $750M + Otezla $550M) — these patients need new homes. Bimzelx is the natural destination for IL-17 class graduates, and BE BOLD gives it a pull factor in PsA that no competitor can match.

  4. BE BOLD superiority data is Bimzelx's biggest competitive asset since launch — the first head-to-head biologic superiority demonstrated against an IL-23 inhibitor. Every competitor earnings call reinforces why this data matters: the IL-23 narrative has been one-sided until now.

5. Competitive Landscape Snapshot

Skyrizi (AbbVie)

  • Still dominant at ~45% US biologic Rx share in psoriasis
  • FY 2025 expected at ~$17.3B globally -- the largest biologic in immunology
  • Cross-indication momentum continues (PsO + Crohn's + UC)
  • BE BOLD creates the first head-to-head data deficit for Skyrizi in PsA
  • AbbVie's commercial firepower (largest pharma sales force in derm/GI) remains formidable

Tremfya (J&J)

  • $5.2B in 2025 sales (+40% YoY)
  • Pursuing UC indication expansion
  • Positioned as the "safe + convenient" IL-23 option with every-8-week dosing
  • Less directly threatened by BE BOLD than Skyrizi (Tremfya not the comparator)

Cosentyx (Novartis)

  • Biosimilar competition approaching -- multiple candidates in late-stage development
  • If biosimilar secukinumab launches at significant discount, it could reshape IL-17 class economics
  • May paradoxically help Bimzelx by increasing IL-17 class familiarity and reducing the "IL-23 is the default" dynamic

Taltz (Eli Lilly)

  • Maintained position as the established IL-17A inhibitor of choice
  • ZoomRx data showed Taltz is the default for "Convert IL-17A Writers" who lack Bimzelx familiarity
  • Lilly's attention increasingly on GLP-1/obesity franchise, potentially reducing dermatology commercial focus

6. ZoomRx Engagement Priorities

Immediate Opportunities

  1. Post-BE BOLD Tracking: UCB will want to know whether BE BOLD data actually changes prescribing behavior. A follow-up HCP-Pt Conversation study (Wave 2) fielded in Q2-Q3 2026 could measure:

    • Are HCPs mentioning head-to-head data in conversations?
    • Has the default product shifted for PsA patients?
    • Are Bimzelx Dabblers converting to Believers?
    • Is the safety perception softening with accumulated real-world experience?

  2. HS Market Intelligence: Bimzelx has been on the market for HS for 16 months. UCB likely needs:

    • HS-specific HCP-Pt Conversations to understand prescribing dynamics in this different patient population
    • Promotional effectiveness tracking for the HS commercial team
    • Competitive assessment vs. Humira biosimilars in HS

  3. Rheumatology Channel Research: BE BOLD data targets rheumatologists directly. ZoomRx's current research focused on dermatologists and NP/PAs. A rheumatology-specific study would capture a new decision-maker audience and provide UCB with insights on a channel where they face established competition from Cosentyx, Taltz, and Rinvoq.

Medium-Term Opportunities

  1. Longitudinal Panel Tracking: Recruiting a subset of the original HCP panel for a 12-month follow-up would create a rare longitudinal dataset showing how prescribing behavior evolves with product maturation, new data (BE BOLD), and accumulated experience. This would be a premium deliverable.

  2. Cross-Indication Integration: As Bimzelx's indication portfolio stabilizes (5 indications), UCB may seek integrated research across PsO, PsA, axSpA, and HS -- understanding how HCPs think about Bimzelx as a platform vs. indication-specific. ZoomRx could propose a multi-indication tracker.

Account Health

  • UCB relationship is strong, with the HCP-Pt Conversation study well-received
  • The behavioral science approach differentiated ZoomRx from standard conversation analysis providers (including Verilogue, the incumbent competitor)
  • UCB's commercial success (EUR 2.23B Bimzelx sales) means budget availability is not a constraint
  • Key risk: UCB may consolidate research vendors or expand internal analytics capability as the brand matures

7. Key Questions for Q2 2026

  1. BE BOLD full data: When will the complete dataset (beyond topline) be presented? Which congress? This determines the timeline for any post-data tracking study.
  2. HS commercial trajectory: Is Bimzelx HS uptake meeting UCB's internal targets? Are there early signals from HS-specific promotional effectiveness?
  3. Access improvement: Has UCB made progress on insurance parity with Cosentyx/Taltz/Skyrizi? The access barrier was a consistent theme in ZoomRx data.
  4. Biosimilar Cosentyx timing: When does the first biosimilar secukinumab enter the market, and how does UCB plan to navigate the IL-17 class dynamics?
  5. ZoomRx renewal: What is the timeline for renewing or expanding the HCP-Pt Conversation study? Is Wave 2 funded?

A rheumatology-specific study would capture a new decision-maker audience and provide UCB with insights on a channel where they face established competition from Cosentyx, Taltz, and Rinvoq.


Medium-Term Opportunities


Longitudinal Panel Tracking: Recruiting a subset of the original HCP panel for a 12-month follow-up would create a rare longitudinal dataset showing how prescribing behavior evolves with product maturation, new data (BE BOLD), and accumulated experience. This would be a premium deliverable.


Cross-Indication Integration: As Bimzelx's indication portfolio stabilizes (5 indications), UCB may seek integrated research across PsO, PsA, axSpA, and HS -- understanding how HCPs think about Bimzelx as a platform vs. indication-specific. ZoomRx could propose a multi-indication tracker.


Account Health

UCB relationship is strong, with the HCP-Pt Conversation study well-received
The behavioral science approach differentiated ZoomRx from standard conversation analysis providers (including Verilogue, the incumbent competitor)
UCB's commercial success (EUR 2.23B Bimzelx sales) means budget availability is not a constraint
Key risk: UCB may consolidate research vendors or expand internal analytics capability as the brand matures


7. Key Questions for Q2 2026
